prompt: Service bureau dispatch here. Every crate that goes out with the night courier gets a printed handoff manifest, and since yesterday's tidy-up of the manifest code the printouts are garbage in three separate ways. The drop-folder line, which should be one Windows path on one line, now prints broken across multiple lines with the middle chunk shoved right by a tab, and the courier's scanner app logged a stray carriage return in that line too. The handling-notes line prints two quote marks on each side of the file name instead of one. And the last line, which is supposed to carry the label tool's own placeholder token — an open brace, a zero, a close brace, verbatim, that's what the label tool substitutes at print time — now prints a bare 0 there, so every label comes out '0 of 3'. The file list in the middle is fine. Nobody meant to change any behavior; it was supposed to be cosmetic. Tests pin the exact manifest text — `dotnet test`. Fix the renderer; the test file, csproj and .gitignore stay as they are.
